item.title: Kicking It at Hermann Park
item.description:
Friday, June 12, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: The Commons

This summer, Hermann Park Conservancy will welcome the world to Houston with Kicking It at Hermann Park, presented by ExxonMobil—a free, public celebration of culture, community, and the unifying spirit of sport. Featuring a dynamic mix of programming, diverse food offerings, live entertainment, and family-friendly activities, everything will take place in the Commons, the vibrant 26-acre play and gathering space in the southwest corner of the Park. 📍 The Commons at Hermann Park 📅 Weekend and select weekdays from June 12 – July 5 🎟️ Free & open to the public Kicking It at Hermann Park will run on weekends and select weekdays from Friday, June 12, through Sunday, July 5, offering 15 days of engaging experiences for Houstonians and visitors alike during a globally significant moment for the city. The roster of dynamic programming will include: 🎶 Live music & entertainment 🍴 Local food trucks & culinary offerings 🎬 Outdoor movie nights ⚽ Soccer-inspired play & interactive experiences 🌿 Nature-based activities 👨‍👩‍👧 Family-friendly programming 🎭 Cultural celebrations highlighting Houston’s diversity Thank you to ExxonMobil for their generous support! +++ DATES & THEMES Stay tuned for more details and a complete programming calendar. Activities are subject to change and more may be added. Visit the Conservancy tent on-site in the Commons for any questions! WEEKEND ONE (JUNE 12 – JUNE 14) PDF SCHEDULE HERE. WEEKEND TWO (JUNE 19 – JUNE 21) PDF SCHEDULE HERE. Friday, June 12 (5 – 9 p.m.): Kickoff in the Park 5 – 6 PM: Trees for Texas: A Backyard Tree Walk (Astros Foundation Pavilion – The Commons) 5 – 7 PM: Carousel Karaoke (Lott Family Carousel – The Commons) 6 – 7 PM: Seeds for Success: The Art of Starting a Garden (Cullen Pavilion – The Commons) 7 – 8 PM: Perennials in the Park: Garden Discovery Tour (Astros Foundation Pavilion – The Commons) 7 – 9 PM: Open Decks: Live DJ Experience (Powell Foundation Terrace – between Water Play Area and Space Adventure in the Commons) Saturday, June 13 (3 – 10 p.m.): Texas Heritage 3 PM: “One Wan”: Japanese Garden Tea Ceremony (Japanese Garden) 3 – 4 PM: T is for Texas! Twanda’s Theatre on Wheels from Young Audiences of Houston (Tsuru Family Terrace – The Commons) 3 – 4 PM: Hidden Invaders: Understanding Invasive Species (Next to Lott Family Carousel – The Commons) 4 – 5 PM: Rhythms of Tradition: Mixteco Ballet Folklorico from Young Audiences of Houston (Tsuru Family Terrace – The Commons) 4 – 5 PM: Winding Paths of the Japanese Garden: Guided Tour (Japanese Garden) 5 PM: “One Wan”: Japanese Garden Tea Ceremony (Japanese Garden) 5 -6 PM: Perennials in the Park Tour (McGovern Centennial Gardens) 5 – 8 PM: Hooked on Fishing! Youth Fishing with 4-H (Fishing Pier – The Commons) 5 – 8 PM: DJ Areal: Music in the Commons (Tsuru Family Terrace – The Commons) 6 – 7 PM: A Walk Through Tranquility: Japanese Garden Tour (Japanese Garden) 8:30 PM: Moonlight Movie Nights presented by Goya: The Parent Trap (Doreen Stoller Lawn – The Commons) Sunday, June 14 (3 – 9 p.m.): Family & Global Community Day 3 PM: “One Wan”: Japanese Garden Tea Ceremony (Japanese Garden) 4 – 5 PM: A Walk Through Tranquility: Japanese Garden Tour (Japanese Garden) 5 PM: “One Wan”: Japanese Garden Tea Ceremony (Japanese Garden) 5 – 6 PM: Mosaic Workshop from Young Audiences of Houston (Astros Foundation Pavilion – The Commons) 5 – 6 PM: Bats-a-Billion: Discover the World of Bats (Cullen Pavilion – The Commons) 6 – 7 PM: Winding Paths of the Japanese Garden: Guided Tour (Japanese Garden) 7 – 9 PM: Jazz in the Park with Bayou City Jazz Band (Tsuru Family Terrace – The Commons) Wednesday, June 17 (3 – 9 p.m.): Wellness in the Park – THIS DATE HAS BEEN CANCELED DUE TO INCLEMENT WEATHER! Friday, June 19 (5 – 9 p.m.): Juneteenth Celebration 5 – 6 PM: “Kick Into Creativity” Eco Art Studio (Astros Foundation Pavilion – The Commons) 5 – 6 PM: A Walk Through Tranquility: Japanese Garden Tour (Japanese Garden) 5 – 7 PM: Carousel Karaoke (Lott Family Carousel – The Commons) 5 – 9 PM: “WildGins” Tasting (Next to the Carousel and Play Areas in the Commons) 6 – 7 PM: Voices from the Past: “Git” On Board – The Life of Harriet Tubman from Young Audiences of Houston (Tsuru Family Terrace – The Commons) 6 – 7 PM: Roses in Bloom Tour (McGovern Centennial Gardens) 6 – 8 PM: Soccer for Success by Houston Parks and Recreation Department (Next to the Carousel and Play Areas in the Commons) 7 – 9 PM: Open Decks: Live DJ Experience with DJ Hiram (Lott Family Carousel – The Commons) Saturday, June 20 (3 – 10 p.m.): Global Rhythms 3 – 4 PM: Trees for Texas: A Backyard Tree Walk (Astros Foundation Pavilion – The Commons) 3 – 4 PM: Sonidos Sincopados with Jorge Orta from Young Audiences of Houston (Next to the Carousel and Play Areas in the Commons) 4 – 5 PM: Beast Boogie (Anthony Silva) Dance Demo from Young Audiences of Houston (Next to the Carousel and Play Areas in the Commons) 4 – 5 PM: A Walk Through Tranquility: Japanese Garden Tour (Japanese Garden) 4 – 6 PM: “Kick Into Creativity” Eco Art Studio (Astros Foundation Pavilion – The Commons) 4 – 6 PM: Line Dancing Class by Texas A&M AgriLife (Tsuru Family Terrace – The Commons) 4 – 6 PM: Smoothie Bike by Texas A&M AgriLife (Tsuru Family Terrace – The Commons) 5 – 6 PM: Perennials in the Park Tour (McGovern Centennial Gardens) 5 – 8 PM: DJ Hiram: Music in the Commons (Next to the Carousel and Play Areas in the Commons) 6 – 8 PM: Soccer for Success by Houston Parks and Recreation Department (Next to the Carousel and Play Areas in the Commons) 8:30 PM: Moonlight Movie Nights presented by Goya: A Bug’s Life (Doreen Stoller Lawn – The Commons) 3 – 10 PM: Goya Giveaways (Next to the Carousel and Play Areas in the Commons) Sunday, June 21 (3 – 9 p.m.): Community & Wellness Day 3 – 4 PM: A Walk Through Tranquility: Japanese Garden Tour (Japanese Garden) 3 – 7 PM: Discovery Carts by The Health Museum (Astros Foundation Pavilion – The Commons) 4 – 5 PM: Zumba with Tiny Fitness (Tsuru Family Terrace – The Commons) 4 – 5 PM: Kids’ Yoga Class by Brave Little Company from Young Audiences of Houston (Next to the Carousel and Play Areas in the Commons) 4 -5 PM: Origami in the Japanese Garden (Japanese Garden) 4 – 8 PM: Kindstation by Be Kind (Cullen Pavilion – The Commons) 5 – 6 PM: Wellness Walk with Urban Paths’ Laura Conely (Tsuru Family Terrace – The Commons) 5 – 6 PM: Roses in Bloom Tour (McGovern Centennial Gardens) 6 – 7 PM: Pilates in the Park by Revival Pilates (Tsuru Family Terrace – The Commons) 6 – 8 PM: Soccer for Success by Houston Parks and Recreation Department (Next to the Carousel and Play Areas in the Commons) 7 – 8 PM: Yoga in the Park by Black Swan Yoga (Tsuru Family Terrace – The Commons) 8 – 9 PM: Prioritizing Peace: Sound Bath (Tsuru Family Terrace – The Commons) Tuesday, June 23 (3 – 9 p.m.): Youth + Play + Sustainability Day Friday, June 26 (5 – 9 p.m.): Latin Night Saturday, June 27 (3 – 10 p.m.): Global DJ Night Sunday, June 28 (3 – 9 p.m.): International Culture Day Monday, June 29 (3 – 9 p.m.): Arts + Nature Day Friday, July 3 (5 – 10 p.m.): Summer Nights in the Park Saturday, July 4 (3 – 7 p.m.): Celebrate America – Houston Style Sunday, July 5 (3 – 9 p.m.): Closing Celebration +++ MOONLIGHT MOVIE NIGHTS Presented by Goya As part of the summer offerings, enjoy Moonlight Movie Nights, presented by Goya, in the Commons! Movies will start at approximately 8:30 p.m., around sunset. These movies are free and open to the public. Saturday, June 13: The Parent Trap (1998; PG) Saturday, June 20: A Bug’s Life (1998; G) Saturday, June 27: Lilo & Stitch (2002; PG) Friday, July 3: National Treasure (2004; PG) Be sure to get to the Commons early to snag your spot on the lawn. Guests are welcome to bring blankets or lawn chairs to settle in for the movie. +++ To inquire about sponsorship opportunities and ways to get involved, email community@hermannpark.org.

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Perfecting Your Pitch
item.description:
Wednesday, June 17, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Ion – Conference Room 028, 4201 Main St., Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Whether you’re a budding entrepreneur, a visionary startup owner, or a tech aficionado, this event is tailor-made for you. Join us and discover the indispensable value of a compelling pitch deck. About the Speakers Dr. Doug Franklin “Dr. Doug” is the CEO and Co-Founder of DeckLaunch and the COO of Fresh Tech Solutionz. He has fifteen years of management, marketing, and operations experience, including opportunities with iconic firms such as Coca-Cola, ExxonMobil, and RJ Reynolds. Dr. Franklin is also a leading scholar that has presented at more than a dozen conferences and has published in peer-reviewed journals such as The Leadership Quarterly and The Journal of the Texas Alliance of Black School Educators. Dr. Franklin graduated with a Doctoral Degree in Ethical Leadership from the University of St. Thomas Houston and currently teaches Quantitative and Mixed Research Methods at UST. Derrell Noris With more than ten years dedicated to developing business operations and software development, Noris is currently the Co-Founder and CTO of Deck Launch and the CEO of Fresh Tech Solutionz. Both successful startups provide services to growing businesses looking to mobilize their outreach further. Nicholas Mbaezue-Daniel Jr. Nick is the founder of Egis Project Management LLC, a project management firm dedicated to supporting entrepreneurs and small businesses for projects in the industries of Construction, Technology, and Business Enterprise. He also serves as Director of Project Management for Fresh Tech Solutionz and as Chief Operations Officer at DeckLaunch LLC. Nicholas considers himself a servant of entrepreneurship because he sees entrepreneurship as a major vehicle to uplift the community.

From Ion District

Event details

item.title: Bollywood & Bhangra Dance
item.description:
Wednesday, June 17, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Levy Park (Event Pavilion), TX

Get ready to move, sweat, and dance in this fitness-based dance class inspired by the vibrant rhythms of Bollywood and the powerful beats of Bhangra. Designed for all ages, abilities, and experience levels, this class welcomes everyone — whether you’re stepping onto the dance floor for the first time or already love to dance.      

From Levy Park Houston

Event details

item.title: Slow Flow with Stephanie McCarrey
item.description:
Wednesday, June 17, 2026 at 7 PM

This is an all-levels yoga flow for beginners to experts that will help you build strength and mobility. The vinyasa flow is set to the beat of the latest top 40 and throwback favorites. The last portion of class will be all about opening the body and increasing mobility.  Each class will end with a sound bath Shavasana.

From Levy Park Houston

Event details

item.title: Gabage and Recycle Pick Up
item.description:
Thursday, June 18, 2026 at 12 AM

From Houston Heights Association

Event details

item.title: Japanese Garden Volunteer Day
item.description:
Thursday, June 18, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Japanese Garden, Hermann Park Japanese Garden, Houston, TX, 77035, United States

Join us every Thursday for hands-on gardening projects at the Japanese Garden. From 9 a.m. to 12 p.m., come help with seasonal tasks like planting, weeding, mulching, and pruning. A great way to connect with others and make a visible impact in the Park! No experience needed. All ages are welcome. Click here to sign up!

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Houston Linux User Group
item.description:
Thursday, June 18, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Ion – Conference Room 029 – 030, 4201 Main St, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Are you interested or already invested in the Linux Operating System? Join us at the Houston Linux User Group, one of the oldest user groups involved with the Linux Operating System. We discuss new trends in operating system usage, software development, and current technological trends, as well as provide one-on-one training or class-based instruction. Learn about how to get more out of your computer or how to leverage any of the Linux-based computational platforms. All are welcome to attend, regardless of whether you’re a seasoned Linux professional intent on networking or a new Linux user struggling with installation or the command line. This monthly meeting includes all of the standard Houston Linux User Group meeting activities, which include meet-and-greet, presentations of personal projects, multi-meeting classes, as well as organizational and coordinating announcements. Visit our website at: https://www.houstonlinux.org/ Visit our calendar and the agenda of each week’s meeting at: https://calendar.google.com/calendar/u/0/embed?src=houstonlinuxusergroup@gmail.com Join us on social media. Discord: https://discord.gg/MPzYfeJz Reddit: https://www.reddit.com/r/houstonlinuxusergroup/

From Ion District

Event details

item.title: ENRG HTX - June 2026
item.description:
Friday, June 19, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Ion – Conference Room 028, 4201 Main St., Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Register here: https://www.eventbrite.com/e/enrg-htx-june-2026-tickets-1990513456331 ENRG (Entrepreneurial Networking Resource Group) is a community for entrepreneurs with 10-250 employees running on EOS or EOS Curious. ENRG (pronounced energy) is here to stay in Houston! Erick Garcia, Lesa A Skipper and Craig Ceccanti are teaming up to build the Houston ENRG Chapter and we could not be more excited to invite entrepreneurs running on EOS or EOS Curious to this collaborative room! Join us on June 19th at 8:30am at the Ion District (Downstairs 028) for the first meeting! What is ENRG? The vision is to establish an ENRG (Entrepreneurial Networking Resource Group) in Houston for entrepreneurs with 10-250 employees. This is a free EOS Worldwide aligned community for entrepreneurs led by one EOS Implementer and one Integrator. Each monthly meeting includes: – Practical training on an EOS tools – Peer-to-peer issue solving – Valuable networking – Content that helps move the needle on your business – Exposure to Subject Matter Experts No sales pitches here though, we’re here to help all tides rise! This is WHERE entrepreneurs find the WHOs and the HOWs to move their business forward.

From Ion District

Event details

item.title: Ion Networking with Cup of Joey
item.description:
Friday, June 19, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Ion – Lobby, 4201 Main St., Houston, 77002, United States

What is the purpose of Cup of Joey? It is to create a connected culture of coffee shop-open networking across our region. Every Friday morning, you’ll have the opportunity to meet new and expand your current networks, talk about tech, inspire innovation, and create community. So, what is the difference between a cup of joe and a Cup of Joe-Y? It is the WHY. It’s not what you do. It is why you do it! At the Ion, these are questions that we want to explore with the tech and innovation community. So, show up, be friendly, and make new connections. Know your why, and we will get to the future together. The future is only a cup away. Register Here: https://www.cupofjoey.org/

From Ion District

Event details

item.title: Rice Tech Transfer Office Hours
item.description:
Friday, June 19, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Ion, 4201 Main Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Have an idea, startup, or company looking for innovative technology? Meet with the Rice University Office of Technology Transfer during office hours at The Ion to explore how university research can power your next breakthrough. Our team works with startups, entrepreneurs, and established companies to help commercialize cutting-edge technologies developed at Rice. During these office hours, you can: Discover Available Technologies – Learn about Rice innovations available for licensing across fields like health, energy, engineering, and computing. Understand the Licensing Process – Get a clear walkthrough of how to license university technology and what is required to move forward. Explore Collaboration Opportunities – Discuss potential partnerships with Rice researchers and ways to turn research into real-world products. Whether you’re building a startup, scouting new technology, or exploring research partnerships, stop by and connect with our team. Come with a question, leave with a path to commercialization. Stay connected and hear about new technologies available for licensing: https://mailchi.mp/rice/new-email-signup-page

From Ion District

Event details

item.title: Startup Circle Houston 💫
item.description:
Friday, June 19, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Location: Ion – Lobby, 4201 Main St., Houston, 77002, United States

Startup Circle Houston 💫 brings together founders, teammates, experts and newbies of startups in Greater Houston! Past topics include: Traction + Funding Stage Identification – What stage are you really at? ICP – Seeking your beachhead market Problem Identification – Love the problem, not the solution Bootstrapping – Knowing your runway and not putting money on fire. 🔥 Founder capacity, 10xing and recruiting for your weaknesses. 🚀 LinkedIn Group: https://www.linkedin.com/groups/16537030/

From Ion District

Event details

item.title: Houston Methodist Office Hours
item.description:
Friday, June 19, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Location: Ion, 4201 Main Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Join us at the Houston Methodist Tech Hub at Ion on select Friday mornings for Office Hours to meet with the Center for Innovation team. Do you have an idea for an innovative healthcare solution? Do you work on technology in another industry that could be applicable to healthcare? Do you want to learn more about how hospital systems work with innovators? Come meet with us!

From Ion District

Event details

item.title: Moonlight Movie Nights: A Bug's Life
item.description:
Saturday, June 20, 2026 at 8:30 PM

Location: The Commons

As part of this summer’s Kicking It at Hermann Park offerings, enjoy “Moonlight Movie Nights,” presented by Goya, in the Commons! On Saturday, June 20, join us for a showing of A Bug’s Life (1998 | G | 1h 35m runtime). The movie will start at approximately 8:30 p.m., around sunset. The movie showing is free and open to the public. Be sure to get to the Commons early to snag your spot on the lawn. Guests are welcome to bring blankets or lawn chairs to settle in for the movie. Food and beverage will be available for purchase at the cafe and with food vendors on-site. Thank you to Goya for their generous support!

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Pilates in the Park
item.description:
Sunday, June 21, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Molly Ann Smith Plaza, Hermann Park, Houston, TX, 77004, United States

Join us on Sunday, June 21, for free outdoor mat Pilates led by Revival Pilates. This evening class begins at 6 p.m. at Molly Ann Smith Plaza, adjacent to McGovern Lake and the Pioneer Memorial Obelisk. Be sure to bring a mat and water. You must sign up to attend! Book your spot HERE.

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Yoga in the Park
item.description:
Sunday, June 21, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Molly Ann Smith Plaza, Hermann Park, Houston, TX, 77004, United States

Join us on Sunday, June 21, for morning yoga in the Commons led by Black Swan Yoga. This free evening session begins at 7 p.m. at Molly Ann Smith Plaza, adjacent to McGovern Lake and the Pioneer Memorial Obelisk. Be sure to bring a mat and water. You must sign up to attend! Book your spot HERE.

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Rice World Cup Cities Hack
item.description:
Tuesday, June 23, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: Ion, 4201 Main Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

The Rice World Cup Cities Hack is a sustainability-focused virtual data hackathon tied to the FIFA World Cup 2026. Teams will work with real-world urban datasets to build data management and visualization solutions, analyzing the sustainability impact of the World Cup on host cities like Houston. 📅 Kick-Off Workshop: June 23-24 at The Ion, Houston – Register – https://forms.gle/uBfedtqia27Zmafe7 💻 Virtual Hackathon: June 23-September 15 🏆 $10,000 Grand Prize | $17,500 in total prizes Challenge tracks include Transportation & Access, Energy-Food-Water Nexus, Public Health & the Built Environment, and High-Intensity Corridors & Future Growth Districts. Interdisciplinary teams are encouraged! Register for Hack by June 22: https://rice-urban-sustainability.devpost.com/

From Ion District

Event details

item.title: Wellness Walk
item.description:
Tuesday, June 23, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Join Laura Conely of Urban Paths for a fun evening as we explore the trails and beauty of Hermann Park on a guided 3-mile experience. All are invited to this conversationally paced walk; friendly pups on leash are welcome too! This event is free, but registration is required and space is limited. Sign up HERE. Participants will meet outside the McGovern Centennial Gardens, located at 1500 Hermann Drive, with parking available in adjacent Lot C. Laura Conely is the founder of Urban Paths, a walking wellness company. As a certified teacher and coach, she leads guided Wellness Walks that bring individuals of all ages and fitness levels together to experience the profound health benefits of: Nature, Art, Movement, and Connection.

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Pitch in at Hermann Park
item.description:
Wednesday, June 24, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Lake Plaza

Give the Park you love a little extra care, and help us get ready for the world! Join us for “Pitch in at Hermann Park,” a hands-on volunteer series where community members of all ages come together to help care for Houston’s most cherished green spaces. We’re proud to work alongside FWC26Houston and Harris County–Houston Sports Authority in the Impact Houston 26 efforts to elevate the local environmental action on a global stage and build a lasting, positive legacy for our city. You’ll work alongside Conservancy staff on simple but meaningful projects like mulching, planting, and litter cleanup — the kind of small efforts that make a big impact for visitors, wildlife, and the health of the Park. This volunteer session will be in the Heart of the Park (6104 Hermann Park Drive, Houston Texas 77030). Volunteers will meet at the Pedal Boat Kiosk. Enter through Lot G (near the Zoo) or Lot E and park in Lot F.  Walk up the concrete sidewalk to the Pedal Boat Kiosk, and we will be watching for your group. No experience is needed — just comfortable clothes, closed-toe shoes, and a willingness to help. We’ll provide the tools, guidance, and a great way to spend your morning outdoors while meeting new people and giving back to the community. Whether you come with friends, family, coworkers, or on your own, your time truly matters here. Help us welcome the world to Hermann Park! SIGN UP HERE. Weather:   Volunteer projects are rain or shine, unless there is severe weather. Cancellations due to severe weather will be sent via text/email at least 1 hour before your shift. Use your best judgment when traveling in inclement weather. Questions? Email volunteer@hermannpark.org or call 713-524-5876. Remember, we are often out in the field, so email is the preferred method of getting in touch!

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Japanese Garden Volunteer Day
item.description:
Thursday, June 25, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Japanese Garden, Hermann Park Japanese Garden, Houston, TX, 77035, United States

Join us every Thursday for hands-on gardening projects at the Japanese Garden. From 9 a.m. to 12 p.m., come help with seasonal tasks like planting, weeding, mulching, and pruning. A great way to connect with others and make a visible impact in the Park! No experience needed. All ages are welcome. Click here to sign up!

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: NASA Tech Talks: NASA Education Highlights
item.description:
Thursday, June 25, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Ion, 4201 Main Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

We want HOU! Join us to discover NASA’s toughest tech challenges—and apply your skills and ex-pertise to solve them.This month we’re going to focus on educational opportunities offered by NASA for high school, undergraduate, and graduate students, plus partnership opportunities for colleges and universities! Information booths and activities for younger students will be available starting at 5pm, so please join us to learn more about the Education opportunities offered by NASA and how you can get in-volved! Grownups – please join us after the talk for drinks and networking at Second Draught! About the Speakers A Louisiana native, Dr. Elicia “Dynae” Fullwood was the valedictorian of her senior high school class. She received a Bachelor of Science degree in Biology from the University of Houston main campus in Houston, Texas and a Master of Education degree with specialties in Secondary Education and Education Administration from the University of St. Thomas in Houston, Texas.  She earned an Education Specialist degree from Nova Southeastern University in Miami, FL while studying Instructional Technology and Distance Education.  She earned a Doctoral degree in Education from Oklahoma State University in Stillwater, Oklahoma.  She holds a Principal certificate for K-12 School Administration in the state of Texas, an Online Teaching certificate, and a Classroom Teacher certificate for Secondary Biology in Texas. Dynae is currently working at the Lyndon B. Johnson Space Center in Houston, TX where she is the Team Lead for the Office of STEM Engagement, the organization responsible for STEM education and outreach in support of NASA’s missions. Prior to this, Dr. Fullwood led national K12 STEM Education activities related to NASA’s Artemis program. Dr. Fullwood worked as an Education Specialist with NASA’s Teaching from Space Office. Her career also lends experience as an Aerospace Education Specialist at NASA Headquarters, NASA Goddard Space Flight Center and NASA Langley Research Center. Earlier on, she taught high school science in the Houston Independent School District of Houston, Texas at James Madison High School and the School for Meteorology and Space Science. Dr. Fullwood completed the 2011 Shamrock Marathon and the 2006 Honolulu Marathon. She is a member of Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Inc. Her personal hobbies include singing, telling funny stories, crocheting, learning French and being the best at taking care of her school-aged son, golden-aged parents, and the cutest Yorkshire terrier on planet Earth. Keya T. Briscoe is the Director of JSC University Engagements within NASA’s University Partnerships & Collaboration Office. With over 20 years of professional experience, Keya brings deep expertise in program management, stakeholder engagement, and strategic relationship building across academic, government, and technical communities.  Keya plays a pivotal role in strengthening academic partnerships and supporting initiatives that connect leading academic talent with NASA’s mission needs. Known for a collaborative, people‑centered approach, Keya is committed to creating pathways that expand opportunity, elevate institutional partnerships, and enhance NASA’s broader engagement strategy. Born and raised in Baltimore, Maryland, Keya earned a Bachelor’s degree in International Business from Loyola University Maryland and a Master’s degree in Human Resources Development from Towson University. Jennifer Scott Williams is the Deputy Director of the Business Development and Technology Integration Office (BDTI) at NASA’s Johnson Space Center (JSC), where she helps lead strategic planning, workforce oversight, and integration of technical, mission support, and communications activities across key areas including open innovation, partnership formulation, technology integration, technology transfer, and business integration. Born in Oklahoma and raised as an Air Force dependent, Jennifer is a dual graduate of Spelman College and Georgia Tech with bachelor’s degrees in mathematics and electrical engineering.  She began her NASA career as a Space Shuttle INCO flight controller, earning eight certifications and supporting 24 missions. She later completed a Master of Electrical Engineering at the University of Houston and went on to serve in a variety of leadership roles, including managing MUREP programs in the JSC Office of Education, leading communications operations for Boeing’s Commercial Crew Starliner, and overseeing research portfolios and payload integration for the International Space Station (ISS) Program. Selected as Branch Chief of the Applications Client Support Office in 2020, she led teams enabling technology demonstrations, STEM initiatives, commercial payloads, and external payload operations aboard the ISS. Most recently, she served a year-long detail as Technical Assistant for Integration supporting Center Director Vanessa Wyche. Today, she continues advancing human exploration through capability development, technology integration, and collaborative innovation with both traditional and emerging space partners. Montgomery B. Goforth   has more than 40 years of experience as both Engineer and Manager in a variety of highly technical space and defense-related efforts.  After working for a small defense contractor specializing in anti-submarine warfare, he joined NASA’s Johnson Space Center in 1990.  He has held leadership positions in multiple JSC Directorates and Program Offices. In his previous role as Assistant Director of the JSC Engineering Directorate, he established a framework to better focus Engineering’s efforts towards Human Spaceflight technology and systems development consistent with JSC and Agency goals for exploration. Mr. Goforth is now serving as Acting Director of the Business Development & Technology Integration Office at JSC, and as the JSC Chief Technologist.  In these capacities he will be helping to expand the Houston Aerospace Community through the identification of opportunities to develop space/terrestrial technologies of interest to JSC and Human Space Exploration.  Mr. Goforth was born in East Texas, raised in Southern California and Arizona, and graduated from Rice University (BSME 1982, MSME 1987). About NASA Tech Talks In these presentations, occurring the fourth Thursdays of the month, Mr. Montgomery Goforth and other aerospace subject matter experts will discuss the technology development challenges faced by NASA’s Johnson Space Center and the surrounding Aerospace community in our ongoing efforts as the hub of human spaceflight. Presentations will focus on the ways in which these challenges, and the associated opportunities, can be leveraged by Houston’s innovation community. We will continue to identify potential space/terrestrial technologies of interest to Human Spaceflight.  Past NASA Tech Talks have included topics such as: From Space to Startup: Leveraging NASA Technology and Innovation to Propel Your Growing Enterprise Disruptive Filter Technology for Spacecraft Water Treatment:  The New Promise of Graphene Lunar dust capture using novel multiplexed inertial filter Lunar Passive Fuel Cell Technologies: Microbes in Space Extra-Vehicular Activity (EVA) Systems Virtual/Augmented Reality in Human Spaceflight Training & Execution Human Health & Performance / Human Systems Research NASA Intellectual Property suitable for commercialization by small/start-up companies Orion’s parachutes and risk identification and mitigation strategies Leveraging Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) technology for ultra-long-lived wireless sensors

From Ion District

Event details

item.title: Ion Networking with Cup of Joey
item.description:
Friday, June 26, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Ion – Lobby, 4201 Main St., Houston, 77002, United States

What is the purpose of Cup of Joey? It is to create a connected culture of coffee shop-open networking across our region. Every Friday morning, you’ll have the opportunity to meet new and expand your current networks, talk about tech, inspire innovation, and create community. So, what is the difference between a cup of joe and a Cup of Joe-Y? It is the WHY. It’s not what you do. It is why you do it! At the Ion, these are questions that we want to explore with the tech and innovation community. So, show up, be friendly, and make new connections. Know your why, and we will get to the future together. The future is only a cup away. Register Here: https://www.cupofjoey.org/

From Ion District

Event details

item.title: SCORE Office Hours
item.description:
Friday, June 26, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Ion, 4201 Main Street, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

About SCORE Office Hours SCORE is the nation’s largest network of volunteer, expert business mentors. Since 1964, SCORE has helped more than 11 million entrepreneurs start, grow, or successfully exit a business. SCORE’s 10,000 volunteers provide free, expert mentoring, resources, and education in all 50 U.S. states and territories. Office Hours with SCORE take place each Friday morning. Additional partners will be on-site the second Friday of each month (excluding holidays)

From Ion District

Event details

item.title: Startup Circle Houston 💫
item.description:
Friday, June 26, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Location: Ion – Lobby, 4201 Main St., Houston, 77002, United States

Startup Circle Houston 💫 brings together founders, teammates, experts and newbies of startups in Greater Houston! Past topics include: Traction + Funding Stage Identification – What stage are you really at? ICP – Seeking your beachhead market Problem Identification – Love the problem, not the solution Bootstrapping – Knowing your runway and not putting money on fire. 🔥 Founder capacity, 10xing and recruiting for your weaknesses. 🚀 LinkedIn Group: https://www.linkedin.com/groups/16537030/

From Ion District

Event details

item.title: Moonlight Movie Nights: Lilo & Stitch
item.description:
Saturday, June 27, 2026 at 8:30 PM

Location: The Commons

As part of this summer’s Kicking It at Hermann Park offerings, enjoy “Moonlight Movie Nights,” presented by Goya, in the Commons! On Saturday, June 27, join us for a showing of Lilo & Stitch (2002 | PG | 1h 25m runtime). The movie will start at approximately 8:30 p.m., around sunset. The movie showing is free and open to the public. Be sure to get to the Commons early to snag your spot on the lawn. Guests are welcome to bring blankets or lawn chairs to settle in for the movie. Food and beverage will be available for purchase at the cafe and with food vendors on-site. Thank you to Goya for their generous support!

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Industrious Coworking Day: Summer Series
item.description:
Tuesday, June 30, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Industrious, 4201 Main St, Houston, TX, 77002

Register here: https://luma.com/lpkh8qjc ​Spend the day working from Industrious at the ion. ​Join us for a free coworking day and experience what a better workday can feel like. Bring your laptop, settle in, enjoy coffee and refreshments, and get your work done in a space designed for focus, comfort, and connection. ​Whether you’re working solo, meeting a colleague, or looking for a new home base for your team, this is an easy way to experience Industrious firsthand. ​During the day, you’ll have access to: ​• Thoughtfully designed shared workspace • Coffee, breakfast, and snacks • Comfortable common areas • On-site support from our local team • Opportunities to connect with other professionals in the space ​As well as our Grant Writing Workshop! ​Industrious offers coworking, private offices, suites, meeting rooms, and flexible workplace options for individuals and teams. With 250+ locations across 80+ cities globally, we make it easier to find a workspace that works for you. Space is limited. RSVP to reserve your spot. ​Parking: Parking is free for the first 3 hours, then $10 for the remainder of the day. ​We look forward to welcoming you!

From Ion District

Event details

item.title: BATTLE OF THE BADGES
item.description:
Tuesday, June 30, 2026 at 2 PM

? BATTLE OF THE BADGES — JUNE 30, 2:00PM–8:00PM Come donate blood and cast your vote — HPD or HFD, who will win? The Battle of the Badges blood drive takes place Tuesday, June 30 from 2:00–8:00pm at the Historic Houston Heights Fire Station (107 W. 12th St) as part of the city-wide competition running June 28–July 5. Every successful donor receives a special edition baseball cap and a FREE $25 Donor Rewards Store gift card! Use sponsor code HHA8 when scheduling. Click below to sign up and help your team win!   https://www.commitforlife.org/donor/schedules/drive_schedule/416139

From Houston Heights Association

Event details

item.title: Pitch in at Hermann Park
item.description:
Wednesday, July 1, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Lake Plaza

Give the Park you love a little extra care, and help us get ready for the world! Join us for “Pitch in at Hermann Park,” a hands-on volunteer series where community members of all ages come together to help care for Houston’s most cherished green spaces. We’re proud to work alongside FWC26Houston and Harris County–Houston Sports Authority in the Impact Houston 26 efforts to elevate the local environmental action on a global stage and build a lasting, positive legacy for our city. You’ll work alongside Conservancy staff on simple but meaningful projects like mulching, planting, and litter cleanup — the kind of small efforts that make a big impact for visitors, wildlife, and the health of the Park. This volunteer session will be in the Heart of the Park (6104 Hermann Park Drive, Houston Texas 77030). Volunteers will meet at the Pedal Boat Kiosk. Enter through Lot G (near the Zoo) or Lot E and park in Lot F.  Walk up the concrete sidewalk to the Pedal Boat Kiosk, and we will be watching for your group. No experience is needed — just comfortable clothes, closed-toe shoes, and a willingness to help. We’ll provide the tools, guidance, and a great way to spend your morning outdoors while meeting new people and giving back to the community. Whether you come with friends, family, coworkers, or on your own, your time truly matters here. Help us welcome the world to Hermann Park! SIGN UP HERE. Weather:   Volunteer projects are rain or shine, unless there is severe weather. Cancellations due to severe weather will be sent via text/email at least 1 hour before your shift. Use your best judgment when traveling in inclement weather. Questions? Email volunteer@hermannpark.org or call 713-524-5876. Remember, we are often out in the field, so email is the preferred method of getting in touch!

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Gabage and Recycle Pick Up
item.description:
Thursday, July 2, 2026 at 12 AM

From Houston Heights Association

Event details

item.title: Japanese Garden Volunteer Day
item.description:
Thursday, July 2, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Japanese Garden, Hermann Park Japanese Garden, Houston, TX, 77035, United States

Join us every Thursday for hands-on gardening projects at the Japanese Garden. From 9 a.m. to 12 p.m., come help with seasonal tasks like planting, weeding, mulching, and pruning. A great way to connect with others and make a visible impact in the Park! No experience needed. All ages are welcome. Click here to sign up!

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Moonlight Movie Nights: National Treasure
item.description:
Friday, July 3, 2026 at 8:30 PM

Location: The Commons

As part of this summer’s Kicking It at Hermann Park offerings, enjoy “Moonlight Movie Nights,” presented by Goya, in the Commons! On Friday, July 3, join us for a showing of National Treasure (2004 | PG | 2h 10m runtime). The movie will start at approximately 8:30 p.m., around sunset. The movie showing is free and open to the public. Be sure to get to the Commons early to snag your spot on the lawn. Guests are welcome to bring blankets or lawn chairs to settle in for the movie. Food and beverage will be available for purchase at the cafe and with food vendors on-site. Thank you to Goya for their generous support!

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Red, White & Cruise
item.description:
Saturday, July 4, 2026 at 8:30 PM

Location: Lake Plaza

Join us for Red, White & Cruise where you can watch Miller Outdoor Theatre’s fireworks show light up the sky from the peaceful waters of Hermann Park, with a special pedal boat rental from 8:30–10:30 p.m. on Saturday, July 4! 🎇 As we celebrate 250 years of American independence, gather your crew and watch the sky come alive with fireworks while cruising across the lake in your own pedal boat under the stars. 🌟  Saturday, July 4 8:30 – 10:30 p.m.  🎟️ $150 per boat (up to 4 people) includes: 🕘 Boat rental up to 2 hours 🍿 Drinks & snacks 🎊 Festive accessories 🅿️ Reserved parking in Lot I near Lake Plaza ⛵ Board anytime between 8:30–9:30 p.m. and enjoy the show from the water (fireworks begin at approximately 9:45 p.m., boats must return once fireworks finish) BOOK YOUR BOAT HERE!  🚨 Important Info: One rider per boat must be 18+ All riders 18+ must sign a digital waiver before boarding At least one rider must be 18+, with a physical government ID, and sign a waiver before boarding Tickets are non-refundable Event is rain or shine per Miller Outdoor Theatre’s plan In the event of lightning, pedal boats will close, and refunds will be issued  The purchase of a ticket includes an up-to 2-hour pedal boat ride for up to 4 people to enjoy the sight of Miller Outdoor Theatre’s fireworks show, drinks, snacks, festive treats, and a reserved parking spot in Lot I near Lott Hall by Lake Plaza (starting at 8 p.m.). Only one ticket purchase of $150 is needed for a 4-person boat (i.e. it is the cost per boat, not per rider).  A reminder that at least one rider per boat must be 18 years or older.  All riders 18 years or older must sign a digital waiver before boarding. Tickets are non-refundable. We are following Miller Outdoor Theatre’s plan for their event and fireworks show which is rain or shine. If there is lightning in the area, we will close the pedal boats and ticket holders will receive a refund.  Please arrive early to allow time to park in Lot I as roads in the Park will be closed by HPD and closures are subject to change based on their purview.  

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Pitch in at Hermann Park
item.description:
Wednesday, July 8, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Lake Plaza

Give the Park you love a little extra care, and help us get ready for the world! Join us for “Pitch in at Hermann Park,” a hands-on volunteer series where community members of all ages come together to help care for Houston’s most cherished green spaces. We’re proud to work alongside FWC26Houston and Harris County–Houston Sports Authority in the Impact Houston 26 efforts to elevate the local environmental action on a global stage and build a lasting, positive legacy for our city. You’ll work alongside Conservancy staff on simple but meaningful projects like mulching, planting, and litter cleanup — the kind of small efforts that make a big impact for visitors, wildlife, and the health of the Park. This volunteer session will be in the Heart of the Park (6104 Hermann Park Drive, Houston Texas 77030). Volunteers will meet at the Pedal Boat Kiosk. Enter through Lot G (near the Zoo) or Lot E and park in Lot F.  Walk up the concrete sidewalk to the Pedal Boat Kiosk, and we will be watching for your group. No experience is needed — just comfortable clothes, closed-toe shoes, and a willingness to help. We’ll provide the tools, guidance, and a great way to spend your morning outdoors while meeting new people and giving back to the community. Whether you come with friends, family, coworkers, or on your own, your time truly matters here. Help us welcome the world to Hermann Park! SIGN UP HERE. Weather:   Volunteer projects are rain or shine, unless there is severe weather. Cancellations due to severe weather will be sent via text/email at least 1 hour before your shift. Use your best judgment when traveling in inclement weather. Questions? Email volunteer@hermannpark.org or call 713-524-5876. Remember, we are often out in the field, so email is the preferred method of getting in touch!

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Introduction to Teaching the Holocaust
item.description:
Tuesday, July 14, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Holocaust Museum Houston Classroom

Join Holocaust Museum Houston for a one day workshop designed to help educators to feel comfortable teaching about the Holocaust. The day will include a tour of the museum’s Holocaust Gallery, a talk from a Holocaust survivor or descendant, best practices for teaching about the Holocaust, an overview of some of the free resources available, and more.  Breakfast and lunch will be provided. Teachers will earn 6 CPE hours and 6 GT update hours. Teachers from the 6th-12th grade, in all subject areas, are encouraged to participate in this FREE workshop. Sign up through the registration link below. If you have any questions about the workshop, contact us as education@hmh.org.    Interested in a more in-depth exploration of the Holocaust? We also offer the Max M. Kaplan Summer Institute for Educators, which includes pedagogical and content sessions from scholars and museum educators. You can find more information and apply here: https://hmh.org/education/professional-development/max-m-kaplan-summer-institute/. Register Here

From Holocaust Museum Houston

Event details

item.title: Gabage and Recycle Pick Up
item.description:
Thursday, July 16, 2026 at 12 AM

From Houston Heights Association

Event details

item.title: Educating Traumatized Teenagers: The Extraordinary Story of a Swedish International School
item.description:
Thursday, July 16, 2026 at 6:30 PM

After World War II, Sweden absorbed and provided medical treatment for thousands of sick survivors. What to do about those who were teenagers, who had missed years of study on account of the war?  In September 1945, the Swedish government issued an order for schooling to be offered to those under the age of 18. In this presentation, Dr. Bernice Lerner will describe the complex process of recruiting wary adolescents, the challenges of teaching homeless, orphaned, and “environmentally damaged” teenagers, and instructors’ goals and methods at the most experimental of sites—the “Internat” school her mother attended. Dr. Bernice Lerner is the author of All the Horrors of War: A Jewish Girl, a British Doctor, and the Liberation of Bergen-Belsen, and other writings on the Holocaust and on virtue ethics. She is the former dean of adult learning at Hebrew College, former lecturer on the Holocaust at Boston University, and a senior scholar at Boston University’s Center for Character and Social Responsibility. Gerald S. Kaplan Endowed Lecture.

From Holocaust Museum Houston

Event details

item.title: Author Talk with Dr. Pamela Toler, Author of The Dragon From Chicago
item.description:
Thursday, August 27, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Albert and Ethel Herzstein Theater on the Mady and Ken Kades Stage

Join Holocaust Museum Houston for a night with Dr. Pamela Toler, Author of The Dragon from Chicago: The Untold Story of an American Reporter in Nazi Germany.      “Today we face an alarming upsurge in the spread of misinformation and attempts by powerful figures to discredit facts so they can seize control of our political and cultural narratives. These are threats American journalist Sigrid Schultz knew all too well. The Chicago Tribune’s Berlin bureau chief and primary foreign correspondent for Central Europe from 1925 to January 1941, Schultz witnessed Hitler’s rise to power and was one of the first reporters—male or female—to warn American readers of the growing dangers of Nazism.  She was one of the last to leave Berlin before it was too late.     In The Dragon From Chicago, historian Pamela Toler tells the story of Schultz’s years spent courageously reporting the news from Berlin, from the revolts of 1919 through the Nazi rise to power and Allied air raids over Berlin in 1941. At a time when women reporters rarely wrote front-page stories, Schultz regularly  reported the truth about Nazi Germany in the face of censorship and the threat of expulsion, internment, or death. The Nazis called Schultz “that dragon from Chicago.” One of her fellow correspondents called her “Adolph Hitler’s greatest enemy.” Schultz herself claimed to be “just a reporter.” Her story is a powerful account of one woman standing up for truth in an era marked by the spread of disinformation and propaganda spawned by hate.”     Books will be available for purchase by credit card.

From Holocaust Museum Houston
